section.title: API keys
section.description: Used by websites + apps to read this site's published content via the public delivery API (/public/v1/*).
section.readOnlyDescription: Only OWNER and ADMIN members can issue or revoke API keys.

status.previewTooltip: This key can read DRAFT entries via ?preview=1

create.title: Create API key
create.description: Give this key a memorable name. You can copy the secret once on the next screen.
create.originsHint: One per line. Leave empty to allow any origin (server-to-server consumers always work).
create.allowDraftHint: Lets the consumer pass ?preview=1 to receive DRAFT entries in addition to PUBLISHED. Only enable for preview/staging deployments — never for a key embedded in a cacheable public production site.

edit.title: Edit "{{name}}"
edit.description: Update name, origin allowlist, and rate limit. The key secret itself cannot be changed — revoke + create a new one to rotate.
edit.originsHint: One per line. Empty = any origin allowed.
edit.allowDraftHint: Lets the consumer pass ?preview=1 to receive DRAFT entries. Disable to revoke draft access without rotating the key.

reveal.title: Your new API key
reveal.description: Copy this now — we won't show it again. Treat it like a password.

revoke.title: Revoke "{{name}}"?
revoke.description: The key will immediately stop working for any consumer. This is non-destructive — the key record stays for audit purposes. Use Delete to remove it entirely.

delete.title: Delete "{{name}}"?
delete.description: This permanently removes the key record. If the key is still active, any consumer using it will lose access. Cannot be undone.

ga.title: Connect Google Analytics
ga.lead: Six steps to get visitor analytics flowing into your site overview. Takes ~10 minutes if it's your first time.
ga.prereq1: You already have a GA4 property created in Google Analytics with the tracking tag installed on the live site.
ga.prereq2: You have access to the Google Cloud Console (the same Google account works for both).
ga.prereq3: You are an OWNER or ADMIN of the Narah site you want to connect.

step1.title: Grab your GA4 Property ID
step1.description: This is the numeric identifier GA Data API uses — different from the Measurement ID (G-XXXXX) that you put on the website.
step1.b1: Open Google Analytics and select your property.
step1.b3: Under the Property column → Property Settings → Property details.
step1.b4: Copy the Property ID at the top of the card (9–10 digit number).

step2.title: Create (or pick) a Google Cloud project
step2.description: The service account lives inside a Cloud project. Any project works — create a fresh one if you don't have a preference.
step2.b2: Click the project dropdown at the top → New Project (or pick an existing one).
step2.b3: Give it a name like "narah-lsp-mice" and create.

step3.title: Enable the Analytics Data API
step3.description: This is the API the CMS will call to fetch reports.
step3.b1: In your Cloud project, open the link below.
step3.b2: Click Enable. Wait a few seconds.
step3.b3: If you see "API enabled", you're good.

step4.title: Create a service account + key
step4.description: Service accounts let the CMS authenticate as a machine — no human OAuth required.
step4.b1: In Cloud Console → IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → + Create Service Account.
step4.b2: Name it (e.g. narah-cms-analytics). Skip the optional Grant access step → Continue → Done.
step4.b3: Click the new service account → Keys tab → Add Key → Create new key → JSON → Create.
step4.b4: A JSON file downloads. Keep it safe — anyone with this file can read your analytics.
step4.warning: Never commit the JSON to git or share publicly. We encrypt it at rest, but the file in your Downloads folder is plaintext.

step5.title: Give the service account access to your GA property
step5.description: Skipping this step is the #1 reason reports fail. The service account can authenticate but cannot see any data until you add it to GA with Viewer role.
step5.b1: Open the downloaded JSON file in a text editor. Copy the full value of the client_email field.
step5.b2: Open Google Analytics → bottom-left Admin (gear icon) → Property column → Property access management.
step5.b3: Top-right blue + button → Add users → paste the service account email.
step5.b4: Role: Viewer (not Marketer / not Analyst). Uncheck "Notify by email" — service accounts can't receive email. Click Add.

step6.title: Paste everything into Narah
step6.description: Final stretch — back to the CMS.
step6.b1: Open Site settings → Google Analytics section.
step6.b2: Property ID: paste the number from step 1.
step6.b3: Service account JSON: open the file from step 4 and paste the full contents.
step6.b4: Click Connect. The site overview will start showing visitor data within a minute.

ga.doneDescription: Head back to settings to paste your credentials. If you just set up tracking today, GA may take 24–48 hours to aggregate the 30-day numbers — realtime works instantly.

t1.fix: Service account email isn't added to the GA property yet. Repeat step 5 and confirm the role is Viewer (not Marketer/Analyst — those don't grant API read on all properties).

t2.fix: Wrong Property ID. Double-check step 1 — make sure you copied the numeric ID from the Property Settings card, not the Measurement ID (G-XXXXX).

t3.fix: Either the site has no traffic yet, or GA hasn't aggregated data yet (24–48h for fresh tracking). Open GA directly to confirm data is flowing.

t4.fix: You pasted something other than the JSON file contents. Open the downloaded file in a text editor and copy everything between { and } including the braces.

analytics.title: Visitor analytics
analytics.notConnectedDescription: Connect a GA4 property to surface visitor and content performance here.
analytics.trendSubtitle: {{users}} users · {{views}} page views over the period

settings.title: Google Analytics
settings.description: Connect a GA4 property using a service account so we can fetch reports on your behalf.
settings.propertyIdHint: Numeric only. Find it in GA → Admin → Property Settings.
settings.serviceAccountHint: Paste the full JSON key downloaded from Google Cloud.
settings.serviceAccountHintConnected: Service account is stored. Paste a new one only if you need to rotate it.
settings.savedSuccess: Google Analytics is connected. Reports will appear on the site overview.

hub.title: Content
hub.description: Every content type defined for this site. Open one to view & edit its entries.
hub.emptyHint: Define your first schema under Configure → Schema.

entries.emptyHint: Click "New entry" to create the first one.

editor.deleteConfirmDescription: This permanently removes the entry. This action cannot be undone.
editor.slugHint: Leave empty to derive from a title-like field.
editor.auditEmpty: Save the draft to see audit info.
editor.noFields: This content type has no fields yet.
editor.addFieldsHint: Add fields in the schema builder.
editor.askAdmin: Ask a super admin to add fields.
editor.viewerNotice: Your role can view but not edit content. Ask an admin to upgrade your access.

revisions.title: Revisions
revisions.empty: No revisions yet. We snapshot the previous state every time you save.
revisions.footer: Last 10 saves are kept. Older revisions are pruned automatically.
revisions.previewBannerDescription: Saved {{when}} by {{author}}. Form is read-only until you restore or close this version.
revisions.previewBannerCurrentTitle: You are viewing the current version (v{{version}})
revisions.previewBannerCurrentDescription: This is the entry as it stands right now — saved {{when}} by {{author}}. Nothing to restore. Close this view to keep editing.
revisions.previewDescription: Saved {{when}} by {{author}}. Compare snapshot ("Then") with current entry ("Now").
revisions.noFields: This content type has no fields.

page.title: Schema
page.description: Define reusable content types — fields, validation, relations — that power every entry in this site.

contentTypes.title: Content Types
contentTypes.description: Define reusable schemas for this site's content.
contentTypes.emptyHint: Create your first schema to start modeling content for this site.

builder.noDescription: No schema description has been added yet.

summary.title: Schema summary
summary.description: Quick reference for this content model.

notes.title: Builder notes
notes.description: Reference the API explorer below before handing off.
notes.body: Establish field names, apiIds, and schema metadata first in the Visual builder tab. The API Explorer below auto-generates the response shape, the TypeScript type, and a cURL example for your frontend team.

apiExplorer.title: API explorer
apiExplorer.description: How the public delivery API will return this content type.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: Define the reusable fields for this schema. Use the arrow controls to adjust ordering.
fields.emptyHint: Start shaping this schema by adding the first field.
